core.label: Groovy Fundamentals
core.description: Syntax, dynamic typing, and GDK enhancements.

topics.label: Installation & groovysh
topics.description: Install Groovy with SDKMAN and use groovysh REPL. Groovy runs on the JVM.

topics.label: Variables & Types
topics.description: def for dynamic typing, optional type declarations. Groovy is optionally typed.

topics.label: Strings & GStrings
topics.description: Single-quoted (Java string), double-quoted (interpolation), triple-quoted (multiline), and slashy strings.

topics.label: Collections
topics.description: Lists ([]), maps ([:]), ranges (..), and sets. Enhanced with GDK methods.

topics.label: Control Flow
topics.description: if/else, switch (supports any type), for-in, while. Groovy switch is more powerful than Java's.

topics.label: Closures
topics.description: Code blocks with { -> } syntax. Closures are Groovy's most distinctive feature.

topics.label: OOP
topics.description: Classes, interfaces, traits, and enums. Similar to Java but with less boilerplate.

topics.label: Operators
topics.description: Safe navigation (?.), Elvis (?:), spread (*.), and spaceship (<=>). Groovy adds many useful operators.

topics.label: Java Interop
topics.description: Call Java seamlessly — Groovy compiles to JVM bytecode. Use any Java library.

topics.label: Testing (Spock)
topics.description: Write expressive tests with the Spock framework — given/when/then blocks.
